titleStmt.title: The Counselor of Embassy in China ( Jones ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Counselor of Embassy in China ( Jones ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 1733. Before his departure,For departure of the Ambassador from China, see vol. viii , “The Embassy in China after occupation of Nanking by Chinese Communists”. Ambassador learned from reliable source of conversation between General Chen YiCommunist mayor of Shanghai. and Chen Ming-shuShanghai representative of the Kuomintang Revolutionary Committee (KmtRC) cooperating with the Communist Party. in Shanghai which may offer some explanation Communist allegations Communist China port blockade is of American origin. (See Shanghai telegram 2906, July 23, repeated Nanking 1634. Vol. viii, “Political and military situation in China”, chapter VI.) Chen Yi said Communist intelligence reported that it was Admiral BadgerVice Adm. Oscar C. Badger, Commander, U.S. Naval Forces in the Western Pacific. who had first suggested idea of blockade to Generalissimo. He added that it was obvious, in light this suggestion, that blockade represented official US policy. Source of report locally was Lo Hai-sha, confidant of Chen Ming-shu and latter’s Nanking representative.

While we are not inclined give credence to this piece Communist intelligence, it may indicate predilection of CCPChinese Communist Party. agents for reporting what they think their superiors would like to hear. Likewise possible this allegation will be introduced into CCP propaganda re blockade at opportune moment.
